cards.back: Physics developed after 1900, including relativity and quantum mechanics.
Einstein's theory relating space and time for observers in uniform motion.

cards.front: What are the **two postulates** of special relativity?
cards.back: 1. Laws of physics same in all inertial frames. 2. Speed of light constant for all observers.
$\Delta t = \gamma \Delta t_0$, where $\gamma = \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-v^2/c^2}}$
Time interval measured in frame where events occur at same position.
$L = \frac{L_0}{\gamma}$, where $L_0$ is proper length.
Mass of an object as measured in frame where it is at rest.
$p = \gamma mv$
$E = \gamma mc^2$
Emission of electrons from a metal when light shines on it.
$hf = K_{max} + \phi$, where $\phi$ is work function.
Minimum energy needed to eject an electron from a metal surface.
Minimum frequency for photoelectric effect: $f_0 = \frac{\phi}{h}$
A quantum (particle) of light with energy $E = hf$.
$h = 6.626 \times 10^{-34} \text{ J}\cdot\text{s}$
Increase in wavelength of X-rays when scattered by electrons.
$\Delta \lambda = \frac{h}{m_e c}(1 - \cos\theta)$
Particles exhibit both wave and particle properties.
$\lambda = \frac{h}{p}$
Interference pattern from electrons, demonstrating wave nature.
$\Delta x \Delta p \geq \frac{\hbar}{2}$ and $\Delta E \Delta t \geq \frac{\hbar}{2}$
Theory describing nature at atomic and subatomic scales.
Fundamental equation of quantum mechanics describing wave function evolution.
Mathematical function describing quantum state of a system.
$|\psi|^2$ gives probability density of finding a particle.
Particle passing through a potential barrier even with energy less than barrier height.
No two fermions can occupy same quantum state simultaneously.
Particles with half-integer spin (e.g., electrons, protons).
Particles with integer spin (e.g., photons).
Discrete wavelengths emitted or absorbed by atoms.
Early quantum model with electrons in quantized orbits: $E_n = -\frac{13.6 \text{ eV}}{n^2}$
Physical quantities can only take discrete values.
Intrinsic angular momentum of a particle.
Study of atomic nuclei and their interactions.
Spontaneous decay of unstable nuclei emitting radiation.

cards.front: What are the **three types of radioactive decay**?
cards.back: Alpha ($\alpha$), beta ($\beta$), and gamma ($\gamma$).
Time for half of radioactive sample to decay.
Splitting of heavy nucleus into lighter nuclei, releasing energy.
Combining light nuclei into heavier nucleus, releasing energy.
